title: The First Crossing
setting: Two Native American men, a shaman and his Chief, sit on a bluff overlooking the ocean. The wind carries salt and memory. The sun is low.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: I have had a series of horrible dreams from the Great Mother.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: Our people being destroyed. Our way of life shoved aside. And damage to the Great Mother.

lines.speaker: Chief
lines.text: You above all know that the Great Mother cannot truly be damaged. She can just correct her vibration and tell us what she needs us to do. Like we have always done since the first light in the void.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: Not this time. She has shown me the destruction of us and our way of life. Our simple way is going to be challenged by strange men from across the big water.

lines.speaker: Chief
lines.text: No one can cross the big water. You have to stay close to the land or risk drowning.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: They are coming. These pale men that have been judged for their faith, their acts. People so victimized they will not see us — they only see their pain.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: If I am to believe what Great Mother has shown me, our strongest and bravest can travel deep into the forest and mountains and commune with Mother, and she will share our companion animal bodies.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: We can switch between man and animal at will, eventually, and will hold the truth of Mother in our hearts.

lines.speaker: Chief
lines.text: You're saying that Mother is going to strip the nations of their strongest and best to save a future none of us will see.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: Yes. But she requires this contrast to continue her expansion. And through that expansion, all men's growth.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: Yes, but more. A true transformation into spirit animals and back again. A cycle of life we have not shared before.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: Just like our companions have helped us hunt, find shelter, and survive — we will now join with their spirits and become more than we are now. But hidden from all.

lines.speaker: Shaman
lines.text: In the care of the Great Mother. Watching over all. And participating only when needed.

postStoryNote: The cave remembers this conversation. It happened here — or somewhere like here — a long time ago. The attunement you are about to begin is part of that same cycle. The Great Mother is still expanding. The companion spirits are still waiting.
